headline: The deadly cheetah trafficking between Somaliland and the Gulf
subhead: The feline is highly sought after by wealthy collectors of wild animals, who are numerous on the Arabian Peninsula. Across the way, in the Horn of Africa, Somaliland has become the main hub of a thriving trafficking network, even as the species itself is under threat.

Macron praises Europe's 'predictability' in contrast to Trump's unpredictability
subhead: The French president was speaking to an audience of Japanese business leaders and investors on Wednesday, the second day of his visit to Japan.

headline: French archives of Spaniards who fled Franco's dictatorship are now available online
subhead: From March 30, over 180,000 records are accessible on the French Office for the Protection of Refugees and Stateless Persons' website, due to funding from Spain aimed at preserving the memory of people's 40 years of exile in France.

headline: Robert Capa, a photographer in the heat of the action, at Paris's Liberation Museum
subhead: A compelling exhibition, which features a montage of archival footage showing the work of Robert Capa, the Hungarian cofounder of Magnum Photos, details his extraordinary life.

headline: On housing, Mayor Grégoire wants people to 'still dream of living' in Paris
subhead: The new mayor has pledged to create 30,000 new social housing units and 30,000 additional 'affordable' homes – an ambitious goal. To reach it, Paris will have to nearly double its annual production of public housing.

'StravaLeaks': How Le Monde located 18,000 French military personnel with a fitness app
subhead: For more than two years, Le Monde has been tracking a significant proportion of France's military personnel on Strava, a popular fitness app, on almost a daily basis. Their movements can be monitored, including in sensitive areas, putting both military operations and the servicemembers involved in them at risk.

headline: War is transforming Ukraine's dogs who live on the front line
subhead: Canines found near combat zones are small, light and dark-colored, according to a study. These characteristics have helped them survive in a hostile environment.

headline: Ireland gives Trump a lesson on Europe
subhead: During the traditional Saint Patrick's Day visit to the White House by the Irish prime minister, Micheál Martin defended his British counterpart and praised the virtues of Europe in the face of criticism from the US president.
